title: Medical Transcriptionists
description: Transcribe medical reports recorded by physicians and other healthcare practitioners using various electronic devices, covering office visits, emergency room visits, diagnostic imaging studies, operations, chart reviews, and final summaries. Transcribe dictated reports and translate abbreviations into fully understandable form. Edit as necessary and return reports in either printed or electronic form for review and signature, or correction.

tasks.text: Return dictated reports in printed or electronic form for physician's review, signature, and corrections and for inclusion in patients' medical records.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Produce medical reports, correspondence, records, patient-care information, statistics, medical research, and administrative material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify mistakes in reports and check with doctors to obtain the correct information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review and edit transcribed reports or dictated material for spelling, grammar, clarity, consistency, and proper medical terminology.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Transcribe dictation for a variety of medical reports, such as patient histories, physical examinations, emergency room visits, operations, chart reviews, consultation, or discharge summaries.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Distinguish between homonyms and recognize inconsistencies and mistakes in medical terms, referring to dictionaries, drug references, and other sources on anatomy, physiology, and medicine.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and maintain medical files and databases, including records such as x-ray, lab, and procedure reports, medical histories, diagnostic workups, admission and discharge summaries, and clinical resumes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Translate medical jargon and abbreviations into their expanded forms to ensure the accuracy of patient and health care facility records.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform data entry and data retrieval services, providing data for inclusion in medical records and for transmission to physicians.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Take dictation using shorthand, a stenotype machine, or headsets and transcribing machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform a variety of clerical and office tasks, such as handling incoming and outgoing mail, completing and submitting insurance claims, typing, filing, or operating office machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Decide which information should be included or excluded in reports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive and screen telephone calls and visitors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Receive patients, schedule appointments, and maintain patient records.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer inquiries concerning the progress of medical cases, within the limits of confidentiality laws.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
